content.problemStatement:
Nazik gave Batman a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). There are some robbers in the tree; of course, Batman wants to catch the robbers.

Initially, Batman is in a vertex sss and his power is equal to ppp. During each of the next MMM seconds, the following process happens:


A robber with power rrr runs from a vertex xxx straight to vertex yyy.
Batman either catches the robber or stays in his current vertex (and never catches this robber).
Batman can only catch the robber if Batman's power is strictly greater than the robber's power.
Let's denote Batman's current vertex by sss (s=sis=s_is=si‚Äč initially). To catch the robber, Batman must choose a vertex ggg such that each vertex on the simple path between xxx and yyy (inclusive) lies also on the simple path between sss and ggg (inclusive). If it is impossible to choose a vertex ggg so that this condition is satisfied, Batman cannot catch this robber.
If Batman catches the robber, ggg becomes his current vertex.
After Batman catches this robber, his power increases by ttt.
Your task is to calculate the maximum power Batman can have after MMM seconds if he chooses the robbers to catch optimally.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1054\cdot10^54‚čÖ105
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1054\cdot10^54‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, sis_isi‚Äč and ppp.
Each of the next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge in the tree between vertices uuu and vvv.
The next line contains a single integer MMM.
Each of the following MMM lines contains four integers xxx, yyy, rrr and ttt describing a robber.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the maximum possible value of Batman's power after MMM seconds.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

In the 2-nd second, Batman will go from vertex s=1s=1s=1 to vertex g=5g=5g=5.
In the 4-th second, Batman will go from vertex s=5s=5s=5 to vertex g=6g=6g=6.
Batman should ignore robbers moving in the 1-st, 3-rd and 5-th second.
